title: Journey begins
description: Pregnancy is measured from the first day of the last menstrual period. Your body is preparing for ovulation and possible pregnancy.
imageDescription: A microscopic view of cells beginning their journey.

title: Ovulation week
description: Ovulation usually occurs this week. If sperm meets the egg, fertilization can happen.
imageDescription: An egg cell released from the ovary.

title: Fertilization
description: Fertilization may occur, creating a zygote. The fertilized egg begins dividing rapidly while traveling toward the uterus.
imageDescription: A fertilized egg dividing into multiple cells.

title: Embryo forming
description: The embryo implants in the uterine wall. The neural tube begins to form.
imageDescription: A tiny embryo nestled in the uterine lining.

title: Heart forming
description: The heart and circulatory system are beginning to develop. Early cardiac activity may start this week. The brain and spinal cord are also developing.
imageDescription: A tiny embryo showing early organ development.

title: Heartbeat detected
description: Cardiac activity can often be detected on ultrasound. The embryo has a distinct head and tiny limb buds are forming.
imageDescription: An ultrasound image showing a tiny heartbeat.

title: Brain development
description: {name}'s brain is growing rapidly. Arm and leg buds are visible and {name} moves spontaneously.
imageDescription: A blueberry-sized embryo with distinct limb buds.

title: Embryo stage
description: Fingers and toes are forming. The heart is beating rapidly and major organs continue developing.
imageDescription: A raspberry-sized embryo with visible fingers and toes.

title: Moving around
description: The fetus can move, though you cannot feel it yet. Muscles and nerves are developing rapidly.
imageDescription: A grape-sized embryo with developing muscles.

title: Becoming a fetus
description: {name} is now officially a fetus. All vital organs are formed and beginning to function.
imageDescription: A kumquat-sized fetus with fully formed fingers.

title: Unique fingerprints
description: {name} is developing unique fingerprints. Tooth buds are forming under the gums.
imageDescription: A fig-sized fetus with developing fingerprints.

title: Fetal stage begins
description: Most major organs are formed. {name} can make small movements and facial expressions.
imageDescription: A lime-sized fetus making subtle facial movements.

title: End of first trimester
description: The risk of miscarriage drops significantly. {name} can suck their thumb.
imageDescription: A pea pod-sized fetus sucking their thumb.

title: Second trimester starts
description: {name} is making breathing-like movements with amniotic fluid. Fine hair called lanugo begins to appear.
imageDescription: A lemon-sized fetus with developing hair follicles.

title: Bones hardening
description: Bones are hardening and {name}'s ears are moving into position. {name} may begin to hear sounds.
imageDescription: An apple-sized fetus with hardening skeleton.

title: Growing rapidly
description: {name} can hear some sounds. The skeleton continues hardening from cartilage into bone.
imageDescription: An avocado-sized fetus with a fully developed skeleton structure.

title: Fat developing
description: {name} is storing fat for warmth and energy. The umbilical cord is growing thicker.
imageDescription: A pear-sized fetus developing fat stores.

title: Baby yawning
description: {name} may yawn, hiccup, and swallow. Some parents begin feeling movements called quickening around this time.
imageDescription: A bell pepper-sized fetus in a yawning pose.

title: Sensory development
description: {name}'s brain is developing areas for the senses: smell, taste, sight, hearing, and touch.
imageDescription: A mango-sized fetus with developing sensory systems.

title: Halfway there!
description: {name} can swallow and is covered in protective vernix. Movements and kicks are usually noticeable now.
imageDescription: A banana-sized fetus covered in protective vernix coating.

title: Taste buds forming
description: {name}'s taste buds are fully formed. {name} can taste the amniotic fluid and may prefer sweet flavors.
imageDescription: A carrot-sized fetus with fully developed taste buds.

title: Looking more human
description: {name} looks more like a miniature newborn. Lips, eyelids, and eyebrows are becoming more distinct.
imageDescription: A spaghetti squash-sized fetus with recognizable human features.

title: Gaining weight
description: {name} is gaining weight steadily. The lungs are developing but are not yet ready for breathing outside the womb.
imageDescription: A grapefruit-sized fetus gaining healthy weight.

title: Viability milestone
description: The lungs are developing rapidly. {name} practices breathing movements by inhaling amniotic fluid.
imageDescription: A corn-sized fetus practicing breathing movements.

title: Responding to sounds
description: {name} responds to familiar voices and music. The startle reflex is developing.
imageDescription: A rutabaga-sized fetus with responsive hearing.

title: Eyes open
description: {name} may begin opening their eyes. The retinas continue developing.
imageDescription: A zucchini-sized fetus with open eyes.

title: Dreaming begins
description: Brain activity is increasing significantly. {name} now has more regular sleep and wake cycles, including REM sleep.
imageDescription: A cauliflower-sized fetus in a peaceful sleep state.

title: Third trimester begins
description: {name} can open and close eyes. Brain activity increases significantly.
imageDescription: An eggplant-sized fetus with active brain development.

title: Head engagement
description: {name} is continuing to gain fat and muscle. Many babies begin moving into a head-down position around this time.
imageDescription: An acorn squash-sized fetus in head-down position.

title: Hair growing
description: {name}'s hair is growing. Bone marrow is producing red blood cells.
imageDescription: A large cabbage-sized fetus with growing hair.

title: Rapid brain growth
description: The brain is developing rapidly, forming billions of important neural connections.
imageDescription: A coconut-sized fetus with rapidly developing brain.

title: Almost there
description: {name} is practicing breathing movements and continuing to gain weight. Fingernails are fully formed.
imageDescription: A squash-sized fetus with fully formed fingernails.

title: Immune system boost
description: {name} receives antibodies from you through the placenta to strengthen the immune system.
imageDescription: A pineapple-sized fetus receiving vital immune support.

title: Getting ready
description: {name}'s central nervous system and lungs are nearly mature. Fingernails reach the fingertips.
imageDescription: A cantaloupe-sized fetus with maturing organ systems.

title: Kidney development
description: Kidneys are fully developed. {name} continues gaining weight and practicing essential survival skills.
imageDescription: A honeydew-sized fetus with fully developed kidneys.

title: Full term approaching
description: {name} is gaining weight rapidly. Most organs are mature and ready for life outside the womb.
imageDescription: A romaine lettuce-sized fetus nearing full term development.

title: Early full term
description: {name} is considered early term. The grasp reflex is strong and fully developed.
imageDescription: A winter melon-sized fetus with a strong grasp reflex.

title: Full term
description: {name} is considered full term soon. Most organs are fully functioning and {name} is settling lower into the pelvis.
imageDescription: A pumpkin-sized fetus fully ready for birth.

title: Any day now
description: {name} has shed most of the lanugo. The placenta continues providing nutrients and antibodies.
imageDescription: A mini watermelon-sized fetus ready to meet the world.

title: Ready to meet you!
description: {name} is fully developed and ready for birth. Labor may begin at any time.
imageDescription: A watermelon-sized baby fully ready for their debut.
